description: Two or more components, formats, or methods are 'thosami' when they can work together for a chosen purpose through a stated interface. A plug and socket may fit physically while their electrical requirements remain incompatible. 'theali' (fitting) judges broader suitability; 'samethu' (equivalent) says that different things give a selected matching result.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', let air pass along it, and round the lips into true-mid back 'o'. Raise the tongue blade towards the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's', direct air through the narrow channel, and open into stressed central 'a'. Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', carry the voice through the nose, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: One component can carry the adjective attributively; after a plural subject, the predicate precedes final 'nai' (be). A 'nua' phrase may identify its counterpart. In noun position, 'thosami' means compatibility. Exact protocol and version names remain in their source form when it would not identify the tested pairing.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A community can keep an old tool in service when a new part meets its interface instead of forcing the whole system into the discard pile.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A claim about technical fit does not quietly decide whether the result is accessible, consensual, or useful to the person involved.

semantic_domains.activity: components working together for a selected purpose
semantic_domains.communication: communication formats able to be exchanged or rendered together
